name: Palette Kota Kinabalu Near by Imago Shopping Mall Formerly Zara's Boutique Hotel
detail: Room is clean and staff is also friendly and helpful.
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map

name: Hotel O Hotel Oriental
detail: The room is spacious enough for 3 of us with very affordable room rate. The location is a bit far to the town compared to other hotels but you may sleep comfortably as the place is quite from those pubs and bars. There is no lift for this hotel, so be prepared if you are travelling with the senior citizens. There is some snacks at the reception to buy in case that you are hungry in the middle of night. :)
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map

name: Grand InHotel Kota Kinabalu
detail: Best hotel in town ...6* for everything ..highly recommended
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map

name: The Shore Kota Kinabalu City Centre by LW Suites
detail: The apartment was modern, clean, and had breathtaking views.
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map

name: Homy Seafront Hostel
detail: The best part about it was the environment of the hostel, they were really comfortable and cozy feel, great after a long day of sightseeing/shopping we had no trouble sleeping! The bedrooms were basic, but had everything you needed for this price point. The wash rooms were very new and clean, and it equipped with hot water, toiletries, and even hair dryer, basically everything you needed for a refreshing shower after a long day of exploring outside. For us the best bit was the location and the price. The location was great for us as we mainly visited the downtown of Kota Kinabalu, it was walking distance to tourist attractions such as waterfront KK, local flea market, and major shopping malls. We actually ended up walking most places, even down to Suria KK and jesselton point, but we did take a grab back! The most annoying thing about walking is having to cross the street every block, as the traffic is kind of busy at the area, which can add a lot of time onto a journey.
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map

name: Mercure Kota Kinabalu City Centre
detail: It is located in Gaya street, so that should not be a problem to travel around the city center on foot. Do take note that you will need the local parking coupon if you wish to park your car in front of the hotel. Despite the small space of toilet and shower room, the room is actually quite spacious and comfy. Overall, this is a satisfied stay in a new international standard hotel in Kota Kinabalu.
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map

name: Horizon Hotel Kota Kinabalu
detail: the horizon hotel in kota kinabalu is situated in the heart of the city close the istanza, the sabah governor’s residence. it’s a new hotel, being only 4 months old. but don’t let that dissuade you from staying here. the general manager, philip d. theseira, who i would now count as a good friend, has a top rate staff. all are very welcoming, polite and most helpful; addressing you by name as to go about your business. they even moved me to a new room, because i couldn’t establish an internet connection in my original room. rooms are spacious having king sized beds, a small area for sitting and relaxing, and large walk in showers. larger rooms are also available for those with children. but the room size i had was perfect for the duration of my stay. the hotel has three levels of secure private parking, which is free to hotel guests. in addition there is a swimming pool and well equipped gymnasium on the sixth floor, allowing for mostly uninterrupted views out across the bay. these are used at the guest’s own risk, as there are no staff or life guards present. the two excellent restaurants, give the guest a choice of food styles from around the region, as well as international cuisine. however please be aware that all food is halal, so there is no pork available on the menus. with its situation in the centre of the city; it’s only a short walk to all the shopping malls, restaurants and bars. i never had to use a taxi once, preferring to walk around and see all that was on offer. it’s also ideally located to all the scuba dive centres that offer very reasonably priced diving off the islands of gaya and sapi. i’ve already planned my next stay, if for nothing else, but the convenience. but that’s not the only reason of course.
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map

name: Promenade Hotel Kota Kinabalu
detail: Very good value for money. Clean, good facilities and fit out is modern and functional. Would recommend to anyone as either business or holiday hotel. Facilities are just as good as some of the more expensive 'Brand' hotels in Kota Kinabalu but at a significantly lower cost.
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map

name: Signel Hostel
detail: For a solo traveler the place is perfect because it is close to everything...just a walking distance to the night market, Sunday gaya market, jesselton port, waterfront, shopping mall, Atkinsons clock tower and Signal Hill observatory tower. The bed is comfortable and safe, I booked in their 8-bed female dormitory, you wouldn't even feel that there were other people because it was quiet so privacy is not an issue. The facilities are well maintained as well. Kudos to the staff especially for being honest and nice. I will be staying in Signel if I'll be vising Sabah again. ^.^
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map

name: Signel Poshtel
detail: The room itself is super clean with amazing interior design.
area: Central Business District,Kota Kinabalu - View on map
